isaa.subtitle: Indian Scale for Assessment of Autism
isaa.description: A standardised 40-item scale developed for Indian children, covering Social, Emotional, Speech, Behavioural, Sensory, and Cognitive domains.

inclen.subtitle: INCLEN Diagnostic Tool for ASD
inclen.description: A diagnostic tool validated for use in Indian settings by INCLEN, supporting early identification of Autism Spectrum Disorder.

iasq.subtitle: Indian Autism Screening Questionnaire
iasq.description: A rapid parent/caregiver-completed screening questionnaire for use in primary healthcare settings as an early triage tool.

cars2.subtitle: Childhood Autism Rating Scale, Second Edition
cars2.description: A widely-used standardised rating scale for assessing autism severity, with standard and high-functioning forms.

isaa.title: ISAA
isaa.subtitle: Indian Scale for Assessment of Autism

instructions.heading: Indian Scale for Assessment of Autism (ISAA)
instructions.institute: National Institute for the Mentally Handicapped · Govt. of India

steps.title: How it is Done
steps.body:
A trained clinician takes 45–60 minutes using:
• Direct observation of behaviours.
• Interaction (tasks, communication testing).
• An interview with the parent/caregiver.

steps.title: The 40-Item Scoring System
steps.body:
Rate 40 behaviours across 6 domains (Social, Emotional, Speech, Behavioural, Sensory, Cognitive) on a 1–5 scale:
• 1 (Rarely): 0–20%
• 2 (Sometimes): 21–40%
• 3 (Frequently): 41–60%
• 4 (Mostly): 61–80%
• 5 (Always): 81–100%

steps.title: Understanding the Results
steps.body:
Scores summed (Min 40, Max 200):
• Below 70 → No Autism
• 70–106 → Mild Autism
• 107–153 → Moderate Autism
• Above 153 → Severe Autism
